rationale: The cited paper is used in a grouped background citation when the authors briefly define the general idea of ideal-point product positioning. The citing paper does not engage with Lee, Sudhir, and Steckel (2002)'s multiple-ideal-point framework, switching structure, or estimation method. Instead, it develops its own generative-design pipeline and HB conjoint-based ideal-point construction, so this citation functions as broad literature context rather than a specific building block.
